content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 2 high-severity integration issues: the new export path does not match the real `br` CLI contracts for `list --all --json` and `create` label flags, so `milestone export-beads` will not run successfully outside the mocks. Targeted bead-export tests passed, and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` plus `cargo fmt --check` passed; a full `cargo test` run still shows an unrelated existing failure in `cli::run::tests::resume_running_failed_project_reenters_failure_reconciliation`.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `materialize_beads()` assumes `br list --all --json` is a bare `Vec<BeadSummary>` at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1526-1530`, and its fallback matcher makes the same assumption at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3671-3680`. On the installed `br` CLI, `br list --all --json` returns an object wrapper (`{"issues": [...]}`), so the export path fails with a parse error before it can check for existing beads or create anything.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Add a wrapper deserializer for `br list` responses (or normalize the envelope in the adapter) and update the tests to use the real `{"issues": [...]}` payload shape.
   Rationale: This blocks the primary `milestone export-beads` workflow in a real workspace even though the mocked unit tests pass.
2. finding_summary: Every export create call includes milestone labels because `materialized_bead_labels()` always prepends `milestone:<id>` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1903-1918`), but `create_bead()` still serializes labels as repeated `--label=...` flags (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:1341-1346`). The installed `br create` command rejects that flag in favor of `--labels`, so the first real create exits immediately and no beads can be materialized.
affected_files: ["src/adapters/br_process.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Serialize labels using the CLI's supported `--labels` contract and add an integration-shaped test that validates the actual command line emitted for labeled creates.
   Rationale: Even after fixing the list-response parsing, the feature still cannot create the root epic against the real `br` binary because every exported bead carries at least one label.
